text:
```json
[
  "Machine learning is a type of technology that allows machines and computers to learn by observation.",
  "Machine learning concepts form the foundation of many modern data-driven technologies.",
  "Machine learning empowers systems to learn from data and make predictions or decisions without explicit programming.",
  "Machine learning concepts are broadly categorized into supervised learning, unsupervised learning, and reinforcement learning.",
  "Machine learning algorithms empower computers to classify data and make predictions based on past data.",
  "Machine learning tries to replicate how humans learn by comparing new information to past experiences.",
  "Supervised learning is a form of machine learning that trains a model on labeled data.",
  "Supervised learning algorithms learn to map inputs (data) to target outputs (the algorithm’s goal).",
  "Common supervised learning algorithms include linear regression, neural networks, support vector machines, and decision trees/random forests.",
  "Linear regression evaluates how numerical data relates on a linear plane.",
  "A neural network attempts to replicate human cognition by adding complex layers of nodes.",
  "A support vector machine classifies data to separate it into distinct categories.",
  "A decision tree/random forest model uses a branching structure of questions to classify data.",
  "Supervised learning applications include predictive analytics, sentiment analysis, and object or image recognition.",
  "Predictive analytics is a form of analysis that predicts what could happen in the future.",
  "Unsupervised learning trains an algorithm on unlabeled data.",
  "Unsupervised learning algorithms independently identify patterns and structures, creating their own labels.",
  "Common unsupervised learning algorithms include principal component analysis, K-means clustering, and probabilistic clustering.",
  "Principal component analysis simplifies input data without losing the most relevant parts of the information.",
  "K-means clustering sorts unlabeled data into smaller groups.",
  "Probabilistic clustering creates clusters and observes how data and clusters exist in space.",
  "Semi-supervised learning combines supervised and unsupervised techniques, using a subset of labeled data to guide the algorithm.",
  "Unsupervised learning can be used to detect anomalies in data, segment customer bases, and power recommendation engines.",
  "Reinforcement learning is a type of unsupervised learning that enables a machine to learn through trial and error.",
  "Reinforcement learning uses a gamified reward system to help a computer determine which method works best.",
  "Reinforcement learning algorithms learn through making decisions.",
  "Reinforcement learning applications include market personalization, optimization, and financial predictions.",
  "Machine learning and deep learning both describe types of AI.",
  "Deep learning is a type of machine learning, specifically a neural network that uses many layers of neural networks.",
  "Deep learning algorithms are sometimes called deep neural networks.",
  "The document was updated on February 19, 2025."
]
```
json_data: Machine learning is a type of technology that allows machines and computers to learn by observation.
Machine learning concepts form the foundation of many modern data-driven technologies.
Machine learning empowers systems to learn from data and make predictions or decisions without explicit programming.
Machine learning concepts are broadly categorized into supervised learning, unsupervised learning, and reinforcement learning.
Machine learning algorithms empower computers to classify data and make predictions based on past data.
Machine learning tries to replicate how humans learn by comparing new information to past experiences.
Supervised learning is a form of machine learning that trains a model on labeled data.
Supervised learning algorithms learn to map inputs (data) to target outputs (the algorithm’s goal).
Common supervised learning algorithms include linear regression, neural networks, support vector machines, and decision trees/random forests.
Linear regression evaluates how numerical data relates on a linear plane.
A neural network attempts to replicate human cognition by adding complex layers of nodes.
A support vector machine classifies data to separate it into distinct categories.
A decision tree/random forest model uses a branching structure of questions to classify data.
Supervised learning applications include predictive analytics, sentiment analysis, and object or image recognition.
Predictive analytics is a form of analysis that predicts what could happen in the future.
Unsupervised learning trains an algorithm on unlabeled data.
Unsupervised learning algorithms independently identify patterns and structures, creating their own labels.
Common unsupervised learning algorithms include principal component analysis, K-means clustering, and probabilistic clustering.
Principal component analysis simplifies input data without losing the most relevant parts of the information.
K-means clustering sorts unlabeled data into smaller groups.
Probabilistic clustering creates clusters and observes how data and clusters exist in space.
Semi-supervised learning combines supervised and unsupervised techniques, using a subset of labeled data to guide the algorithm.
Unsupervised learning can be used to detect anomalies in data, segment customer bases, and power recommendation engines.
Reinforcement learning is a type of unsupervised learning that enables a machine to learn through trial and error.
Reinforcement learning uses a gamified reward system to help a computer determine which method works best.
Reinforcement learning algorithms learn through making decisions.
Reinforcement learning applications include market personalization, optimization, and financial predictions.
Machine learning and deep learning both describe types of AI.
Deep learning is a type of machine learning, specifically a neural network that uses many layers of neural networks.
Deep learning algorithms are sometimes called deep neural networks.
The document was updated on February 19, 2025.
